hero_subtitle: SFTY connects KYC and KYB providers to streamline onboarding workflows with automated routing and approval for low-risk customers while preserving a full audit trail.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Onboarding slows when low-risk routing lags
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to approve low-risk customers efficiently when risk-based routing relies on manual checks and disconnected provider outputs. This delays onboarding and weakens the audit trail for critical approval decisions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs into a unified workflow that applies policy-driven risk rules to route low-risk customers for automatic approval while preserving a full audit trail.
workflow_title: From onboarding checks to low-risk approval
workflow_description: SFTY aggregates KYC and KYB provider checks, applies risk-based rules, routes reviews, and moves approved low-risk customers through structured onboarding decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond KYC outputs. Controlled approval workflow
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs into structured risk-based routing, approval evidence and audit-ready onboarding workflows without replacing existing tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with controlled low-risk approval workflows, preserving audit-grade evidence and enabling secure enterprise deployment for trusted onboarding operations.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support low-risk onboarding approval routing
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how SFTY structures risk-based KYC and KYB workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence supports low-risk onboarding decisions
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY connects provider outputs for compliance reviews
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for low-risk onboarding?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures onboarding workflows by connecting KYC, KYB, sanctions, and risk signals, enabling controlled low-risk customer approvals with audit-ready decision records.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY route low-risk approvals?
faq_2_answer: SFTY applies policy-driven risk rules to route low-risk customers through structured approval review paths, reducing manual review while preserving compliance controls.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve onboarding evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves comprehensive onboarding evidence, including provider outputs, decision logs and audit trails, supporting review readiness and traceability for each approved customer.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect existing KYC and KYB providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates multiple KYC and KYB providers, orchestrating their outputs into connected risk-based workflows without disrupting existing compliance systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC or KYB providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements KYC and KYB providers by turning their outputs into structured workflows and does not replace any underlying service.
final_title: Structure low-risk onboarding with audit trails
final_description: Review how SFTY connects KYC and KYB outputs into controlled workflows for automated low-risk approval with full evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
